Existing IS methods can be divided into two major categories: two-stage and single-stage methods.
Two-stage methods could produce higher segmentation accuracy compared to single-stage methods.
Two-stage methods cost more computational resources than single-stage methods.
Some 'hard' samples with irregular shapes like 'person' are assigned larger masks.
The 'easy' samples like 'frisbee' are assigned smaller masks.

The proposal-based two-stage methods, specifically those which follow a detection-and-segmentation paradigm, are still considered the top performers.
Mask R-CNN first generates a group of proposals with an object detector.
Mask R-CNN then performs per pixel foreground/background segmentation on the Regions of Interest (RoIs).

node_2: excessive predictions on the smooth regions
edge: High-resolution masks often generate excessive predictions on the smooth regions, leading to high computational complexity.
Objects with irregular shapes demand more fine-grained masks to predict, known as 'hard' samples.
In comparison, objects with regular shapes and fewer details can be efficiently segmented using coarser masks.
Objects with irregular shapes require more fine-grained masks to predict, referred to as 'hard' samples.
Objects with regular shapes and fewer details can be segmented using coarser masks, referred to as 'easy' samples.
The adaptively adjusting the mask size for each instance is proposed to improve IS performance by assigning high-resolution masks to 'hard' objects and low-resolution masks to 'easy' objects.
The mask size is adjusted differently for 'easy' and 'hard' objects to reduce computation for 'easy' samples and improve accuracy for 'hard' samples.
The adjustments in mask size aim to reduce redundant computation for 'easy' samples while improving accuracy for 'hard' samples, achieving a balance between accuracy and speed.

Directly predicting a high-resolution mask using Mask R-CNN unexpectedly degrades the mask average precision, which is attributed to the oversimplified mask head and extraction of RoI features from coarse pyramid levels.
The RoI features of larger objects are extracted from higher pyramid levels with coarse downsampling operations, leading to limitations in increasing mask size for these RoIs.
The mask grid size is gradually enlarged in the proposed dual-level FPN framework to make more precise mask predictions, overcoming the oversimplification issue in Mask R-CNN.

In addition to traditional image-level FPN, a region-level FPN is designed in the proposed framework to achieve coarse-to-fine mask prediction by constructing information flows between them at different pyramid levels.
Information flows are constructed between i-FPN and r-FPN at different pyramid levels to incorporate complementary context for mask prediction in the proposed framework.

Information flows between i-FPN and r-FPN at different pyramid levels, aiming to incorporate complementary contextual and detailed information from multiple feature levels for high-quality segmentation
Dual-level FPN framework is developed for IS, with direct information flows from i-FPN to r-FPN at multiple levels, facilitating complementary information aggregation from multiple pyramid levels

DynaMask proposes a dynamic mask selection method to adaptively assign appropriate masks to different instances, assigning low-resolution masks to 'easy' samples for efficiency and high-resolution masks to 'hard' samples for accuracy
